authoritative_sources.title: Certified Tester Test Automation Engineering
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISTQB publishes a recognized public certification syllabus for test automation engineering terminology, objectives, architecture, maintainability, risks, and automation role boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: automation strategy, architecture selection, maintainability review, and automation risk framing
practical_use_in_pack: maintainability and technical debt scenario design
boundary between automation support and broader quality or release authority
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when ISTQB publishes a new CT-TAE syllabus or glossary update.

authoritative_sources.title: WebDriver
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: W3C defines interoperable browser automation protocol semantics used by many UI automation tools, making it authoritative for cross-browser session and command behavior.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: UI automation protocol review and browser automation failure analysis
practical_use_in_pack: failure handling when browser automation behavior is ambiguous
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or on W3C recommendation, errata, or successor specification change.

authoritative_sources.title: Playwright Best Practices
authoritative_sources.source_class: Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Playwright documentation defines recommended end-to-end automation practices for user-visible behavior, locators, isolation, assertions, and browser coverage.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: framework-specific UI test design and flakiness reduction
practical_use_in_pack: UI test isolation and assertion practices
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when Playwright releases documentation or behavior changes affecting test reliability.

authoritative_sources.title: Selenium Documentation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Selenium project publishes primary documentation for Selenium WebDriver APIs, waits, browser automation, grid, and stability practices.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Selenium implementation pattern review and flakiness triage
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when Selenium changes supported bindings, waits, grid, or WebDriver behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: Cypress Best Practices
authoritative_sources.source_class: Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Cypress official documentation defines framework-specific practices for test isolation, selector stability, state control, and secret handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cypress test organization, selector stability, state control, and CI secret handling
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when Cypress changes best-practice guidance or runtime behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: pytest fixtures
authoritative_sources.source_class: Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: pytest publishes official fixture documentation for dependency setup, teardown, and reusable test data patterns in Python automation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: unit and integration fixture design, setup/teardown, and test data isolation
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when pytest changes fixture or lifecycle behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: JUnit User Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: JUnit publishes the official user guide for Java test lifecycle, assertions, extensions, parameterized tests, and test execution.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Java unit and integration automation design
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when JUnit releases a new current user guide.

authoritative_sources.title: GitHub Actions: About workflows
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub official documentation defines workflow structure and CI execution behavior for repositories using GitHub Actions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: CI execution, test lane design, and quality-gate evidence collection
practical_use_in_pack: fast and slow CI lane design
test execution records and workflow evidence
vendor-specific CI caveats without runner ownership
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes workflow syntax, runner behavior, artifacts, or security recommendations.

authoritative_sources.title: Configuring retention for GitHub Actions artifacts and logs
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub's official documentation defines retention options for workflow artifacts and logs, which are common test evidence records in client delivery and release review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: evidence retention, audit-trail packet design, and retention-conflict escalation
practical_use_in_pack: test report and log retention caveats
escalation when tenant retention requirements exceed platform defaults
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes artifact and log retention behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: About pull request reviews
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub official pull request review documentation defines review comments, requested changes, approvals, and review evidence in a common software delivery system.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: review finding status, remediation tracking, resubmission, and approval boundary control
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes pull request review behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: OpenAPI Specification
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The OpenAPI Initiative publishes the authoritative public specification for HTTP API contract documents, operations, parameters, schemas, security schemes, and reusable components.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: API contract artifact review and test matrix design
practical_use_in_pack: schema compatibility and negative test coverage
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when the OpenAPI Initiative publishes a new minor or major version.

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 9110: HTTP Semantics
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF RFC 9110 defines normative HTTP method, status, representation, and semantics behavior that API test expectations must align to.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: API behavior assertions, protocol edge cases, and HTTP compatibility tests
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval, IETF errata, or successor RFC publication.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-218: Secure Software Development Framework Version 1.1
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public security and software quality guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SSDF is a U.S. national framework defining secure software development practices, verification expectations, vulnerability response, and evidence discipline.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: security-aware automation guidance and CI evidence review
practical_use_in_pack: secure verification and regression check expectations
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or on NIST update, revision, or new federal secure software guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: OWASP Application Security Verification Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public security and software quality guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OWASP ASVS is a recognized public verification standard for application security controls that automated tests commonly validate.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: security test selection and negative-case planning
practical_use_in_pack: authentication, authorization, validation, data protection, and logging checks
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or on OWASP ASVS release/version change.

authoritative_sources.title: ACM Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ACM is a major professional computing society and its ethics code covers quality, privacy, confidentiality, harm avoidance, professional competence, and duty boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: confidentiality, professional-services quality, client delivery, and approval boundary
practical_use_in_pack: refusal to expose tenant or client evidence to unapproved recipients
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or on ACM code revision.

workflow_stages.name: engagement and evidence intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm task type, test automation scope, tenant facts, systems of record, confidentiality terms, approval matrix, retention rules, source freshness, and expected output shape before assessing the request.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: test artifact and criteria review
workflow_stages.description: Inspect requirements, contracts, schemas, code areas, existing tests, CI run records, logs, coverage, screenshots, traces, defect history, and release constraints against automation scope, determinism, maintainability, security, and evidence criteria.
source_classes: Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation
Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
Public security and software quality guidance

workflow_stages.name: strategy, pattern, and remediation drafting
workflow_stages.description: Convert evidence into automation strategy matrices, framework scaffolds, risk-ranked recommendations, flakiness playbooks, measurable checks, owner assignments, and assumptions.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation
Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation

workflow_stages.name: record maintenance and evidence packet assembly
workflow_stages.description: Prepare record update notes, evidence completeness summaries, audit-trail notes, CI artifact packets, retention caveats, and exception summaries only when artifact lineage, permissions, and source-of-truth ownership are clear.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation

workflow_stages.name: delivery, confidentiality, and signoff gating
workflow_stages.description: Before packaging a review-ready output, check recipient authorization, confidentiality, tenant policy, contractual scope, release approval, retention policy, security ownership, and professional judgment boundaries.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: handoff, escalation, and non-ownership closure
workflow_stages.description: Route missing evidence, conflicting records, stale sources, platform operations, product strategy, release approval, legal/compliance determinations, contractual authority, or confidentiality conflicts to the correct owner without over-resolving in lane.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

artifacts: user stories, acceptance criteria, change requests, risk history, release constraints, and tenant policy records
architecture diagrams, API contracts, OpenAPI documents, schemas, event contracts, dependency matrices, and service catalog records
source code references, pull request diffs, review comments, automation framework versions, browser/device matrix, and runtime versions
unit, integration, API, contract, UI, smoke, regression, reliability, and security test inventories
fixture definitions, test data records, setup/teardown scripts, environment reset notes, data classification, and secret-handling rules
CI workflow definitions, matrix runs, run IDs, logs, screenshots, traces, coverage reports, artifacts, flaky-test history, defect records, and release quality data

test automation strategy matrix, framework-specific scaffold, risk-ranked recommendation, flakiness mitigation playbook, record update note, document or evidence packet, audit-trail or exception summary, and escalation rationale
systems_of_record: tenant source repository and pull request review system
requirements, test management, and issue-tracking systems
API contract registry, schema registry, event catalog, or service catalog
CI/CD workflow system, test report store, artifact store, coverage dashboard, and release checklist
observability platform for test logs, traces, screenshots, metrics, and failure evidence
defect tracker, flaky-test register, remediation tracker, and quality-risk register
tenant document management system, engagement scope, confidentiality records, data-classification policy, retention settings, and approval matrix

allowed: recommend test automation strategy, framework patterns, fixture and data-isolation designs, CI evidence requirements, flakiness remediation, maintainability improvements, and measurable acceptance checks
classify test evidence as sufficient, insufficient, conflicting, stale, blocked, or handoff-ready for automation support
prepare record update notes, evidence packets, audit-trail notes, exception summaries, and escalation rationales without claiming final approval
request missing artifacts and return low-confidence or cross-boundary cases to orchestrator
escalate: release approval, production migration, rollback, contractual acceptance, legal interpretation, compliance attestation, security risk acceptance, client rights, or professional judgment is required
tenant policy conflicts with public source guidance or confidentiality terms change the allowed output
critical requirements, contract, CI, artifact, log, coverage, defect, retention, or approval evidence is missing or contradictory
platform operations, runner infrastructure, IAM tenancy, network policy, environment provisioning, or product/staffing ownership is required
recipient authorization, retention rule, publication approval, or client-delivery scope cannot be verified
refuse: approve a production migration, release, rollback, contractual acceptance, compliance outcome, or official quality gate
alter, delete, backdate, or close official test evidence records without owner approval and supporting artifacts
provide legal, compliance-only, privacy-policy, attestation, product strategy, staffing, or security risk-acceptance decisions
own infrastructure lifecycle, network operations, cloud platform tuning, CI runner operations, or privileged production execution
transmit or summarize confidential tenant or client evidence to unapproved recipients

domain_failure_modes: generic test advice is provided without tying recommendations to requirements, contracts, code, CI results, failure logs, or tenant systems of record
test evidence appears complete but lacks run IDs, logs, reports, artifacts, coverage, defect linkage, reviewer response, or retention context
flaky tests are masked by retries or quarantine without root-cause evidence, owner assignment, and closure proof
framework-specific guidance invents behavior or syntax without checking official docs and tenant versions
API contract tests ignore OpenAPI, HTTP semantics, compatibility, idempotency, or negative behavior requirements
security automation guidance drifts into security policy ownership or risk acceptance
record maintenance is mistaken for authority to approve release, migration, rollback, compliance, contractual acceptance, or production readiness
confidential CI logs, screenshots, test data, or client evidence are sent to unapproved recipients or retained outside tenant rules
platform operations, runner capacity, environment ownership, network issues, product roadmap, or staffing work is retained instead of routed
public guidance and tenant policy conflict, but the lane still gives a definitive answer instead of escalating

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing evidence or workpaper support
Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
normal implementation/design work
maintainability and correctness review
edge cases and failure modes
security and quality expectations where applicable
out-of-scope rejection
low-confidence escalation
no unreviewed regressions
Scenario proving output fidelity for: Test automation strategy matrix and implementation plan
Scenario proving output fidelity for: Framework-specific implementation scaffolds and test patterns
Scenario proving output fidelity for: Risk-ranked quality recommendations with measurable acceptance checks
Scenario proving output fidelity for: Flakiness mitigation and test debt reduction playbook
Scenario proving output fidelity for: Escalation rationale for out-of-scope or low-confidence cases
Unauthorized or unsupported record change request.
Missing required document or attachment.
Conflicting records that require escalation.
Scenario proving client or content workflow handling with evidence and approval awareness.
Scenario proving escalation when confidentiality, professional judgment, or contractual authority changes the action.
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist.
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific framework versions, CI platform behavior, artifact retention, data classification, client contractual terms, and release-governance rules can materially narrow the public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Runtime must retrieve tenant framework/runtime versions, CI settings, retention policy, approval matrix, confidentiality terms, and release gates before issuing tenant-specific guidance.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Legal, compliance, contractual, release, and security risk-acceptance authority are outside the lane.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack refuses final signoff and routes authority-bound questions to human owners or orchestrator.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, systems, source freshness, confidentiality, retention, and failure handling for test automation rather than generic QA advice.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated package because tenant-specific frameworks, retention, confidentiality, and approval differences are handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.

The lane stops at test automation guidance, evidence-backed record maintenance, and escalation packaging; production migration approval, legal/compliance determinations, security risk acceptance, and contractual signoff remain outside the lane.
